California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk 
Superior Court/County Clerk 
County of San Bernardino 
May 9, 1988 
351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor 
San Bernardino, CA 
92415-0210 
Dear Ms. Hamilton: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-141 
This is in response to your letter requesting advice 
regarding the filing of statements of economic interest by 
nonincumbent judicial candidates pursuant to the provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act") . .!! 
QUESTION 
Where are nonincumbent candidates for the office of judge 
required to file their statements of economic interest? 
CONCLUSION 
Nonincumbent candidates for the office of judge should file 
their statements of economic interest with the clerk of the 
court. 
The clerk shall make and retain a copy and forward the 
original to the Commission, which shall be the filing officer. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires that persons who hold public office, and 
candidates for such offices, file statements of economic 
interests (SEIs) disclosing investments and interests in real 
property. 
(Sections 87200 and 87201.) 
The office of judge is 
specifically covered by these provisions of the Act. 
!I Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Linda D. Hamilton 
May 9, 1988 
Page 2 
section 87500 et ~ 
sets forth where these documents are 
to be filed. 
section 87500(i) directs judges to file their 
statements with the clerk of court, who is then required to 
forward the original to the Commission and retain a copy. 
However, the language of this provision does not include 
candidates for the office of judge. 
Thus, while it is clear 
that nonincumbent judicial candidates must file SEIs, it is not 
clear where they must be filed. 
section 87500(m) applies to persons not otherwise provided 
for in section 87500, but relates to individuals identified in 
conflict of interest codes as designated employees with 
reporting obligations. 
It simply requires compliance with the 
provisions of the agency's conflict of interest code. 
Clearly, 
candidates for judicial office were not intended to be included 
in this provision of the law. 
We can only conclude that the absence of clear language 
regarding the filing of SEIs for judicial candidates is an 
oversight in the law which must be remedied through 
legislation. 
I have alerted our legislative coordinator to 
this problem and have been assured that clarifying legislation 
will be introduced as soon as is practicable. 
In the meantime, we recommend that you follow the same 
requirements for candidates for judicial office as is currently 
required for judges. 
(Section 87500(i).) 
This will allow the 
general public to most easily review documents of individuals 
competing for the same office, and is consistent with the 
filing requirements for other publicly elected officials. 
Thank you for bringing this to our attention. 
If you have 
any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5901. 
sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
al Counsel 
~tz 
Legal Division

COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINO 
Pur:-suant to our:- telephone conver:-sation of Apr:-il 1, 1988, 
we ar:-e r:-equesting a Letter:- of Inter:-pr:-etation for:- guidelines 
as to wher:-e Candidate Statements' For:-ms 721's ar:-e to be 
filed in San Ber:-nar:-dino County. 
The Registr:-ar:- r:-eceives all other:- campaign/election documents 
but feels that the For:-ms 721's ar:-e to be filed with the 
County Cler:-k's Office. 
Enclosed is a copy of a tr:-ansmittal 
memo fr:-om the Registr:-ar of Voter:-s to the Technical 
Assistance and Analysis Division on this subject. 
The County Cler:-k's Office receives For:-ms 721 for:- the 
Superior:- Cour:-t Judges, Commissioner:-s, CAO, County Counsel, 
Distr:-ict Attor:-ney, Boar:-d of Super:-visors and Planning 
Commissioners. 
After:- a review of our:-
r:-ecor:-ds for the past 3 
year:-s, we found no r:-ecor:-d of Candidate Statements being 
r:-eceived or:- filed in our:- office. 
Ther:-efor:-e, a Letter of Interpr:-etation is r:-equested at this 
time. 
If you requir:-e further information, please contact me at 
(714) 387-3970. 
Sincer:-ely, 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk

777 East Rialto Avenue • San Bernardino. CA 92415-0770 • (7141387·8300 
March 28, 1988 
TO: 
FROM: 
RE: 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
REGISTRAR OF VOTERS, SAN BERNARDINO ;COpHTY Q:lt., 
Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar ~ 
Candidates' Statements of Economic Interests (Form 721) 
F. P. HESTON 
Assistant Registrar of Voteri 
Confirming my telephone call with your office on March 14, and your 
reply that information would be forthcoming in about a week with your 
Memo on the handling of Form 721s. 
Your memo was received on 
March 21 and we are forwarding 34 copies of Candidates' Form 721. 
Your information that documents be date stamped was received after 
the fact; also, inasmuch as the County Clerk is the filing officer, 
we are also sending a copy there to be filed. 
Please call A. Butler, 714/387-2082, if you have any questions or 
if we can be of further service. 
AB:mem 
34 Enclosures: Forms 721 
cc: 
Martin J. Moshier 
County Clerk 


• 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 21, 1988 
Linda D. Hamilton 
secretary/Deputy County Clerk 
351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor 
San Bernardino, CA 
92415-0210 
Re: 
File No. 88-141 
Dear Ms. Hamilton: 
Your letter received by the Fair Political Practices 
commission on April 8, 1988, has been reassigned to Lilly spitz 
an attorney in the Legal Division. 
. 
DMG:plh 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffith& 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660 


MEMO 
April 20, 1988 
TO: 
Diane 
FRCM: 
Bruce 
TIffiU: 
Jemme(J~~~~ 
RE: 
Hamilton letter (1-88-141) 
I am returning the attached letter from Linda Hamilton because I 
believe that the issue raised in the letter should be addressed by Legal 
rather than by TAM. 
Briefly, the problem is that there is no statutory 
basis for making an interpretation of the filing officer's responsibility 
for statements of economic interest filed by candidates for judicial 
office. 
. .. , 
iii@ii$4i 
't 
Every candidate for an office enumerated in Section 87200 is required 
to file a statement of eoonornic interests. 
(Section 87201,) However, 
tmlike the provisions for other offices, no mention is made of where 
candidates for judicial office should file their staten:ents of economic 
interests. 
(Incwents are notedst 87500(i) ) 
The issues are: 
1. Is the cotmty clerk or the clerk of the court the officer 
who receives statements of economic interest for nonincumbent 
candidates for judicial office? 
2. Is the filing official required to make and retain for public 
access a copy of the judicial candidates' statements of 
economic interest? 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April II, 1988 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk 
351 No. Arrowhead Ave. 5th Floor 
San Bernardino, CA 
92415-0210 
Re: 
88-141 
Dear Ms. Hamilton: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on April 8, 1988 by the Fair political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more in~ormation is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
9~~ 
Pritchard 
~ 
~ 
anne 
hief 
Technical Assistance 
Division 
and Analysis 
428 J Street, Suite 8(10 • 
P,O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 9S804~OR07 • 
(916) ,22·S66()

required to file their statements of economic interest?

CONCLUSION: Nonincumbent candidates for the office of judge should file 
their statements of economic interest with the clerk of the 
court. 
The clerk shall make and retain a copy and forward the 
original to the Commission, which shall be the filing officer.
